description: Students explore the importance of learning French, reviewing a brochure on reasons to continue French studies and testing their comprehension with true/false activities.
Students compose an email to persuade their friends of the importance of continuing to study French, drawing on a document about the advantages of French studies.
This activity focuses on hobbies and pastimes, with students exploring vocabulary for various interests and activities and reflecting on their own favourite pastimes.
Building on hobby vocabulary, students sort words into familiar words, cognates, and new words to develop comprehension strategies around leisure activities.
Students examine examples of French-language local TV and radio programs for teens and reflect on the kinds of shows they enjoy.
Students explore the importance of diversity in Canada through a video about Michaëlle Jean and prepare to interview someone not born in Canada about their experience.
Students explore Canadian diversity and how newcomers adapt, listening to an interview with Sanja and writing an article describing the experience of someone not born in Canada.
Students explore a Quebec school timetable, practising vocabulary for schedules and school subjects through matching and sorting activities.
Students explore the transition from Grade 8 to secondary school, working with vocabulary about schedules, subjects, and school staff roles.
Students examine popular Canadian sports that have Indigenous roots, brainstorming examples and learning related vocabulary.

french_title: Honorons les racines de nos sports
description: Students survey a variety of sports with Indigenous origins and take a closer look at one in particular: lacrosse.
Students explore reading comprehension strategies that help with understanding texts, using a dialogue between siblings to discover and practise these strategies.
Students explore the world of film, learning cinema vocabulary, film genres, and how to read and write favourable or unfavourable movie reviews.
Students explore ecological problems threatening the Earth, starting with vocabulary about the planet's beauty and natural riches.
Students consider why recycling matters in the face of growing waste, watching a video on the purpose of recycling and learning related vocabulary.
Students explore the history of cinema, reviewing film vocabulary and genres and considering which film-industry jobs interest them.
Students explore the Paralympic Games, which allow athletes with physical disabilities to achieve great sporting performances on the international stage, supported by a video and vocabulary.
Students explore how a poster communicates through images, colours, and text, examining a sports-themed poster promoting a multi-sport approach.
Students explore the newspaper article and francophonie in their communities, learning journalism terms and analyzing a photo for information.
Students explore written correspondence, especially letters, learning related vocabulary and texting abbreviations and reflecting on their own communication habits.
Students explore the wide variety of French accents and expressions through a video, reflecting on the richness this brings to the language.
Students explore francophone communities, focusing on five European countries with large French-speaking populations: France, Switzerland, Luxembourg, Belgium, and Monaco.
Students reflect on television, reviewing film/TV vocabulary and examining French-Canadian series such as 'L'effet secondaire.'
Students explore and participate in conversations, focusing on the distinction between formal and informal conversations and the use of 'tu' versus 'vous.'
